name: The Immaculate Conception

phase_0.boundary_condition: the Assumption — Mary's bodily entrance into heaven, sealing the Immaculate Conception's trajectory

VINIT.justification: Void state: unborn, not yet existing in time, prior to any stain or grace. The absolute beginning before the Incarnation's shadow reaches backward.

TANCH.justification: Terminal anchor: the closed boundary that contains and validates the entire Immaculate Conception. Her bodily ascension is the hermetic seal proving the doctrine's completion.

AFWD.chosen_element: Divine grace flowing backward through time
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation of Mary's conception toward sanctity. Grace moves from Christ's redemption retroactively into her moment of conception, ascending toward perfection.

AREV.justification: Contravariant: the reverse morphism of corruption that would normally flow into all human conception. This descent is arrested and inverted at Mary's conception.

CLINK.chosen_element: The chain of generations from Adam to Mary
CLINK.justification: Composition: sequential chaining of human lineage. Each generation is linked; Mary's conception is the point where this chain is redeemed and reconstituted without the stain.

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity/self-reference: Mary is herself — the one who knows herself as immaculate, the self-recognition of perfect purity. She is the stone that knows itself.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: Mary's conception splits into two branches: [the natural human conception process] and [the supernatural preservation from original sin]. One event, two simultaneous realities.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Mary's unified nature: fully human, fully preserved
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (natural humanity + supernatural grace) reconstitute the original input — Mary's single, indivisible conception. FFUSE(FSPLIT(Mary's conception)) = Mary's conception.

EVALT.chosen_element: The affirmation of Mary's immaculate state
EVALT.justification: True/affirmative branch: the positive evaluation that Mary was preserved from original sin. The T-arm of the split, anchored by grace's success.

EVALF.chosen_element: The counterfactual: Mary subject to original sin
EVALF.justification: False/negative branch: the negative evaluation — what would have been if grace had not intervened. The F-arm of the split, the path not taken.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The paradice of Mary's dual nature: creature and immaculate
ENGAGR.justification: Both simultaneously: Mary is fully human (subject to natural law) AND fully preserved (subject to supernatural grace). This paradox is held without resolution — she is both at once, the albedo stage of theological transmutation.

IFIX.chosen_element: The dogmatic definition of the Immaculate Conception (1854)
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: the permanent, irreversible record. Pope Pius IX's declaration is append-only, cannot be undone. The doctrine is fixed in the Church's memory forever.

phase_2.split_input: One moment: Mary's conception in time
split_outputs: Natural branch: Mary as human, descended from Adam, subject to natural generation
Supernatural branch: Mary preserved by divine grace, exempt from original sin's stain
phase_2.fuse_result: One person: Mary, fully human yet fully immaculate — the two branches reconstitute her single, indivisible conception

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Before Mary's conception: the void of non-existence, the unborn soul, the moment before grace reaches backward through time
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Mary's immaculate state affirmed: preserved from original sin, full of grace, the successful branch where supernatural intervention succeeds
phase_3.false_description: 10 — The counterfactual negation: Mary subject to original sin, the path of natural corruption that grace prevents
phase_3.both_description: 11 — The paradice: Mary is simultaneously fully human (natural) and fully immaculate (supernatural), both states held without resolution in her unified person

steps.domain_action: Mary's soul before conception — void, unborn, prior to any stain or grace
Divine grace flows backward through time from Christ's redemption toward Mary's conception
Original sin's descent into humanity — the corruption that would naturally flow into all human conception
The chain of generations from Adam through the patriarchs to Mary — each link in the lineage of humanity
Mary's conception splits into two simultaneous branches: [natural human conception] and [supernatural preservation from sin]
The affirmative branch — Mary is preserved, grace succeeds, immaculacy is affirmed
Forward morphism on the T-arm — grace actively transforms the natural conception into the immaculate conception
The negative branch — the counterfactual where Mary would be subject to original sin if grace had not intervened
Reverse morphism on the F-arm — the descent of sin that is arrested and inverted at Mary's conception
The paradice emerges — Mary is held simultaneously as both fully human and fully immaculate, the paradox unresolved
The two branches reconstitute Mary's single, unified nature — fully human yet fully preserved
Mary's identity as Theotokos — she is herself, the self-recognition of immaculate purity, the stone that knows itself
The chain of generations is redeemed — Mary's immaculate conception reconstitutes the lineage without stain
The dogmatic definition (1854) — the doctrine is fixed in the Church's permanent record, append-only, irreversible
The Assumption of Mary — her bodily entrance into heaven seals and contains the entire Immaculate Conception, the hermetic boundary that validates all that came before

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Theological reasoning and Marian devotion — the human faculty that translates the mystery of the Immaculate Conception into doctrine and prayer
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Liturgical commemoration and Eucharistic presence — how the Church communicates the reality of Mary's immaculacy across time and space
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Sacred Tradition and Scripture — the deposit of faith that stores and retrieves the doctrine of the Immaculate Conception across generations
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The liturgical calendar, especially the Feast of the Immaculate Conception (December 8) — what determines the rhythm and timing of commemoration
phase_5.alfs_store: The Marian dogmas of the Church — the canonical reference store: Immaculate Conception (1854), Assumption (1950), and the perpetual virginity of Mary

phase_6.cycle_cost: Contemplative attention and theological precision — the cognitive and spiritual work required to hold the paradox of Mary's dual nature without collapsing it
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Humanity under original sin, the lineage of Adam corrupted, the natural order subject to death and decay
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Mary immaculate, the lineage redeemed at its apex, the natural order touched by grace and elevated toward the supernatural
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because grace does not add disorder; it reorders the existing creation. Mary's immaculacy is not a new thing introduced but the restoration of what should have been — the redemption of the natural order through supernatural intervention, a perfect reconstitution where FFUSE(FSPLIT(x)) = x.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → AREV → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → CLINK → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: The Immaculate Conception
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(4, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑗  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑾 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def the_immaculate_conception_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Mary's soul before conception — void, unborn, prior to any stain or grace)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑗)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Divine grace flows backward through time from Christ's redemption toward Mary...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [2] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Original sin's descent into humanity — the corruption that would naturally fl...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑚)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The chain of generations from Adam through the patriarchs to Mary — each link...)
  -- FSPLIT [4] (gran := 𐑚) (Mary's conception splits into two simultaneous branches: [natural human conception] and [supernatural preservation from sin]) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (The affirmative branch — Mary is preserved, grace succeeds, immaculacy is aff...)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism on the T-arm — grace actively transforms the natural concept...)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (The negative branch — the counterfactual where Mary would be subject to origi...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism on the F-arm — the descent of sin that is arrested and inver...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The paradice emerges — Mary is held simultaneously as both fully human and fu...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The two branches reconstitute Mary's single, unified nature — fully human yet fully preserved)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Mary's identity as Theotokos — she is herself, the self-recognition of immacu...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The chain of generations is redeemed — Mary's immaculate conception reconstit...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The dogmatic definition (1854) — the doctrine is fixed in the Church's perman...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The Assumption of Mary — her bodily entrance into heaven seals and contains t...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def the_immaculate_conception_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (the_immaculate_conception_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def the_immaculate_conception_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (the_immaculate_conception_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem the_immaculate_conception_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
